---
title: "Assignment9Lattice"
author: "Vashay Carpenter"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
# --- MODULE 9: THE GRAPHICS TRIAD ---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#install.packages("lattice")
#install.packages("ggplot2")

library(lattice)
library(ggplot2)

```



```{r cars}
# Load Dataset
data(mtcars)
# Ensure legends and facets show "V-Engine" instead of "0" across ALL plots.
mtcars$vs <- factor(mtcars$vs, labels = c("V-Engine", "Straight-Engine"))
```



```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
#1. BASE R:
# Low control, high speed. The "Quick Audit."
plot(mtcars$hp, mtcars$mpg, 
     col = mtcars$vs, pch = 19,
     main = "Base R: HP vs MPG",
     xlab = "Horsepower", ylab = "Miles Per Gallon")
legend("topright", legend = levels(mtcars$vs), col = 1:2, pch = 19)

hist(mtcars$mpg, breaks = 10, col = "lightgray", 
     main = "Base: Distribution of MPG", xlab = "MPG")

#2.  LATTICE:
# Strength: Trellis graphics and small multiples. 
xyplot(mpg ~ hp | vs, data = mtcars,
       main = "Lattice: Conditioned HP vs MPG",
       type = c("p", "r"), # Points and Regression line
       xlab = "Horsepower", ylab = "Miles Per Gallon")


#3.  GGPLOT2:
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = hp, y = mpg, color = vs)) +
  # Map 'size' ONLY to points to prevent geom_smooth from dropping it
  geom_point(aes(size = wt), alpha = 0.7) +
  # Use linewidth=1 to fix the deprecation warning
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(title = "ggplot2: Multivariate Efficiency Paradox",
       subtitle = "Horsepower vs MPG (Size = Vehicle Weight)",
       x = "Horsepower", y = "Miles Per Gallon",
       size = "Weight (1000 lbs)", color = "Engine Type") +
  theme_minimal()

```
